voiceover_style: confident, practical, clear, tech-savvy but not corporate

scenes.visual: A small business owner sits at a desk in a modest office, looking at an outdated website on a laptop. The scene feels relatable and focused, not gloomy.
scenes.style_notes: realistic cinematic lighting, clean composition, shallow depth of field, no readable text, no logos
scenes.voiceover: Most small companies do not need a bigger team first.

scenes.visual: A desktop workspace with missed customer messages, loose notes, an old contact form, and multiple browser tabs suggesting a disorganized customer flow.
scenes.style_notes: organized enough to understand, realistic office details, no readable text, no logos
scenes.voiceover: They need a website that helps customers take action.

scenes.visual: A clean modern website interface on a desktop monitor, with clear visual sections implying booking, contact, quote request, service cards, and chat support.
scenes.style_notes: premium but practical UI, modern monitor, soft reflections, avoid readable UI copy, no logos
scenes.voiceover: A smart website answers questions, captures leads, books appointments, and builds trust.

scenes.visual: The same small business owner checks a clean dashboard on a tablet or laptop and sees new customer inquiries represented by simple visual cards.
scenes.style_notes: optimistic, calm, realistic, warm practical lighting, no readable text, no logos
scenes.voiceover: It works while the owner is busy doing the real work.
scenes.on_screen_text: Your site should work for you.

scenes.visual: A polished modern small-business website glows on a monitor in a clean office. The composition feels confident, useful, and ready to launch.
scenes.style_notes: crisp realistic tech-business look, restrained glow, sharp edges, no readable text, no logos
scenes.voiceover: For a small company, a smart website is not decoration. It is infrastructure.
scenes.on_screen_text: Smart websites for serious small businesses.
